title: Trichomanes pseudonymanii
intro: Trichomanes pseudonitens (not pseudonymanii), also known as the cave maidenhair, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Trichomanes pseudonitens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, this plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infusate, and sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Trichomanes pseudonymanii is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is a member of the genus Trichomanes, which comprises approximately 300 species of delicate, often tiny ferns. Trichomanes pseudonymanii is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The classification of this species places it within the kingdom Plantae, the division Pteridophyta, and the class Polypodiopsida. As part of the family Hymenophyllaceae, it is closely related to other species of filmy ferns.
